channel.title: Howtos on WordPress FAQ
channel.description: Recent content in Howtos on WordPress FAQ

item.title: Essential WordPress Plugins for Performance and SEO
item.description: Community consensus suggests prioritizing caching plugins and query monitoring tools to resolve performance bottlenecks effectively. Based on 6 community reports. Linked sources: 6. Known Issues Overloading sites with too many plugins Poorly optimized database queries Conflicts between caching and dynamic content Community Q&A What are the best plugins for WordPress performance? Experts recommend using caching plugins like W3 Total Cache or LiteSpeed Cache, alongside Query Monitor to identify slow database queries.

item.title: How Much Does a WordPress Website Cost?
item.description: WordPress costs range from minimal hosting fees for DIY projects to significant investments for professional development and custom integrations. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Variable pricing based on hosting tiers Hidden costs of premium plugins Developer labor costs for complex builds Community Q&A Is a WordPress website free to build? While the WordPress software is free, you must pay for domain registration, web hosting, and potentially premium themes or plugins.

item.title: How to Add an Admin User to WordPress
item.description: Adding an admin user is best performed through the WordPress dashboard to ensure proper user role assignment and database integrity. Based on 0 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues Incorrect database table prefix Insufficient user permissions Cache interference after user creation Community Q&A How do I add a new admin user in WordPress? Navigate to Users > Add New in your WordPress dashboard, fill in the required details, and select ‘Administrator’ from the Role dropdown menu.

item.title: How to Change WordPress Site Title and Tagline
item.description: The site title and tagline are primarily managed in General Settings, though SEO plugins often override these values for search engine results. Based on 4 community reports. Linked sources: 5. Known Issues Theme settings overriding general settings Changes not reflecting in Google search results immediately SEO plugin configuration conflicts Community Q&A Where do I change my WordPress site title? Navigate to Settings > General in your WordPress dashboard to update the Site Title and Tagline fields.

item.title: How to Choose a Domain Name for WordPress
item.description: Choose a short, memorable, and brandable domain name that avoids hyphens and uses a reputable registrar for long-term stability and SEO success. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Choosing a domain that infringes on trademarks Selecting a name that is difficult to spell or pronounce Overlooking domain renewal costs after the first year Community Q&A What makes a good domain name for WordPress? A good domain name is short, easy to remember, avoids hyphens or numbers, and clearly reflects your brand or website niche.

item.title: How to Choose a WordPress Theme
item.description: Community consensus suggests prioritizing lightweight, stable themes like Astra or Enfold over feature-bloated options to ensure better site performance. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues Theme bloat causing slow page load times Compatibility issues with page builders Over-reliance on heavy frameworks Community Q&A What is the most important factor when choosing a WordPress theme? Performance and stability are critical; avoid themes that are overly feature-rich as they often introduce unnecessary bloat that slows down your site.

item.title: How to Create a Child Theme in WordPress
item.description: Creating a child theme is the recommended best practice for modifying themes to ensure your custom changes are preserved during parent theme updates. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Using @import in CSS is deprecated Functionality should ideally be in a plugin Community Q&A What is a WordPress child theme? A child theme is a theme that inherits the functionality and styling of another theme, known as the parent theme, allowing you to make modifications.

item.title: How to Customize WordPress Themes Without Coding
item.description: Users can effectively customize themes without coding by utilizing visual page builders like Divi or Astra’s built-in hook and customizer features. Based on 6 community reports. Linked sources: 6. Known Issues Over-reliance on page builders can impact site performance Limited flexibility compared to custom PHP/CSS solutions Community Q&A Can I customize a WordPress theme without knowing PHP? Yes, most modern themes like Astra or Divi offer visual customizers and drag-and-drop page builders that require zero coding knowledge.

item.title: How to Fix WordPress Performance Issues Caused by Plugins
item.description: Excessive use of plugins is the primary cause of WordPress site slowdowns, which can be mitigated through careful selection and managed hosting. Based on 2 community reports. Linked sources: 2. Known Issues Plugin bloat Unoptimized database queries Poor hosting infrastructure Community Q&A Do too many plugins slow down WordPress? Yes, installing too many plugins can increase server load and page load times, especially if they are poorly coded or conflict with each other.

item.title: How to Install a WordPress Theme
item.description: Installing a WordPress theme is best achieved via the Appearance menu in the dashboard or by uploading a zip file through the theme installer. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Permission errors on managed hosting Theme file size upload limits Compatibility issues with newer WordPress versions Community Q&A How do I install a new WordPress theme? Navigate to Appearance > Themes > Add New, then click Upload Theme to install a zip file or search the official directory.

item.title: How to Install SSL Certificate on WordPress
item.description: The most reliable way to install an SSL certificate is to request it directly from your hosting provider to ensure proper server-side configuration. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Mixed content errors after SSL installation Database URL mismatch in wp_options Redirect loops in .htaccess Incorrect server_name in vhost configuration Community Q&A How do I install an SSL certificate on WordPress? The easiest method is to ask your hosting provider to install it for you, as they manage the server-side security protocols.

item.title: How to Install WordPress: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Manual installation provides the most reliable foundation for long-term site performance and security. Based on 6 community reports. Linked sources: 6. Known Issues Potential server configuration conflicts Database connection errors during setup File permission issues on shared hosting Community Q&A What are the basic requirements to install WordPress? You need a web server running PHP version 7.4 or higher and MySQL version 5.7 or higher, along with HTTPS support. Can I install WordPress on my local computer?

item.title: How to Migrate WordPress to a New Hosting Provider
item.description: Using dedicated migration plugins or manual database and file transfers is the most reliable way to switch hosting providers successfully. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues Database URL mismatch after migration PHP version compatibility errors Email server configuration issues File permission restrictions Community Q&A How do I move my WordPress site to a new host? You can migrate by manually moving your files via FTP and exporting your database via phpMyAdmin, or by using a migration plugin like WPvivid.

item.title: How to Set and Customize WordPress Permalink Structures
item.description: Use the built-in Post Name permalink setting for optimal SEO and stability, avoiding complex custom rewrite rules whenever possible. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 9. Known Issues 404 errors on product categories htaccess configuration conflicts SEO ranking loss during structure changes Custom rewrite rule conflicts Community Q&A How do I change my WordPress permalink structure? Navigate to Settings > Permalinks in your WordPress dashboard and select your preferred structure, such as Post Name, then click Save Changes.

item.title: How to Set Up WordPress for the First Time
item.description: Community consensus suggests prioritizing security, clear site messaging, and performance optimization from the very first installation. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Poor value proposition messaging Security vulnerabilities in default setups Performance bottlenecks with heavy page builders Community Q&A What is the first step in setting up a WordPress site? The first step is choosing a reliable hosting provider and installing WordPress, followed by configuring basic security settings to prevent common vulnerabilities.

item.title: Understanding WordPress: How It Works and Core Concepts
item.description: WordPress is a powerful, flexible CMS that requires careful plugin management and database optimization to maintain high performance and security. Based on 8 community reports. Linked sources: 8. Known Issues Database bloat from excessive plugin usage Performance bottlenecks due to unoptimized PHP queries Compatibility conflicts between third-party plugins Community Q&A What is WordPress? WordPress is a versatile content management system (CMS) that allows users to build and manage websites, ranging from simple blogs to complex ecommerce stores.

item.title: WordPress for Beginners: Getting Started Guide
item.description: Beginners should focus on learning core programming languages like PHP or JavaScript while utilizing managed hosting to simplify the initial setup. Based on 5 community reports. Linked sources: 5. Known Issues Over-reliance on third-party plugins Security vulnerabilities from outdated themes Steep learning curve for custom development Community Q&A How do I start learning WordPress as a beginner? Start by understanding the basics of PHP and HTML, then experiment with a local WordPress installation to learn how the back-end functions.

item.title: WordPress Post-Installation Configuration Checklist
item.description: A systematic approach to post-install configuration ensures long-term site stability, security, and optimal performance for your WordPress installation. Based on 6 community reports. Linked sources: 6. Known Issues Default media library bloat Database query bottlenecks Plugin-related performance degradation Community Q&A What are the first settings to change after installing WordPress? Start by setting your permalinks, configuring site visibility settings, removing default plugins, and setting up a robust backup and security solution.
